introSection: La Marche du mille-pattes (March of the Millipede in English), originally known as Petites Montagnes Russes, is a steel terrain kiddie coaster manufactured by Arrow Development that is located at La Ronde in Montréal, Québec, Canada.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride opened with the park in 1967 as Petites Montagnes Russes. It was renamed in 2005.

The ride is the only remaining roller coaster from the park's opening.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: For the 2005 season, the track was repainted yellow. It used to be brown upon opening.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The train is designed to resemble a millipede.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Marche du Mille-pattes on the park's website

fullText:
La Marche du mille-pattes (March of the Millipede in English), originally known as Petites Montagnes Russes, is a steel terrain kiddie coaster manufactured by Arrow Development that is located at La Ronde in Montréal, Québec, Canada.

The ride opened with the park in 1967 as Petites Montagnes Russes. It was renamed in 2005.

The ride is the only remaining roller coaster from the park's opening.



For the 2005 season, the track was repainted yellow. It used to be brown upon opening.

The train is designed to resemble a millipede.


 * Marche du Mille-pattes on the park's website
